publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-07-12 18:55:26 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, subject files, audiocassettes, manuscript, book.
did.abstract: Correspondence, subject files, audiocassettes, and other materials documenting the shift from gay liberation to gay and lesbian activism, and concerning the establishment of organizations, programs, and issues at Cornell and in Ithaca, including the Cornell University Library's Human Sexuality Collection, Ithaca's Fair Practice Ordinance, the adoption of Local Law C, the addition of sexual orientation to the University's Equal Opportunity statement, and the development of AIDS awareness and policy.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

list.head: Periodicals Separated from #7615:
list.item: Choice: Spring, Winter 1986; Spring-Winter 1987; Spring 1988; Spring, Fall-Winter 1989; Spring, Fall 1990; Winter 1991
IMN Newsletter (Ithaca Men's Network): Aug., Oct. 1985; Aug., Nov. 1989
Journal of Sex Education and Therapy: Fall/Winter 1981
Men's Work: Dec./Jan. 1986/1987; Jan. update, Mar./Apr., Jun. 1987
Resources, Research and Reviews: Mar. 1986; Jun., Oct. 1987; Feb., Apr., June, Sept. 1988; Feb. 1989; Feb., Apr. 1990
GLAAD Bulletin: Jan./Feb., Oct./Nov. 1989; Mar./Apr.-Sep./Oct. 1990; Jan./Feb. 1991; Pride Special and Jul./Aug. 1991-Nov./Dec. 1991; Mar./Apr.-May/Jun. 1992; Sep./Oct.-Nov./Dec. 1992
News from GAYPAC/GAYPAC News: Dec. 1979; Feb., Apr. 1980; Oct.-Dec. 1983; Feb.-Mar. 1984

Outlines: Dec. 1984; Feb. 1985 (2 different issues); Apr. 1985; Jun. 1985; Apr. 1986; Nov./Dec. 1986; Apr./May 1987; Summer 1987; Dec. 1987/Jan. 1988; Apr./May 1989; Sep. 1989; Sep./Oct. 1990; June 1992
AIDS Surveillance Monthly Update: Center for Disease Control, 1990

list.head: Monographs Separated from #7615:
list.item: Blank, Joani, The Playbook for Men about Sex San Francisco, Down There Press, 1975
HIV/AIDS: U.S. AIDS cases reported through January 1991 U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, 1991
Making Colleges and Universities Safe for Gay and Lesbian Students: Report and Recommendations of the Governor's Commission on Gay and Lesbian Youth Boston, 1993

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Michael J. Ellis papers, #7615. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Resident Director, Ecology House; Director of Robert Purcell Union; Associate Dean and Director of Willard Straight Hall, Cornell University, 1976-1993. Director of HIV Counseling and Testing, Whitman-Walker Clinic in Washington,DC, 2000-2004.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, subject files, audiocassettes, and other materials documenting the shift from gay liberation to gay and lesbian activism, and concerning the establishment of organizations, programs, and issues at Cornell and in Ithaca, including the Cornell University Library's Human Sexuality Collection, Ithaca's Fair Practice Ordinance, the adoption of Tompkins County's Local Law C, the addition of sexual orientation to the University's Equal Opportunity statement, and the development of AIDS awareness and and an AIDS policy at Cornell. Also files on sexuality-related and men's conferences, 1985-91 and on the Ithaca Alternative Spiritual Community, and additional files on Ellis' professional and activist life.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Audio visual media that has been digitized: TR-17118 to TR-17122.

controlaccess.subject: AIDS (Disease)
Gays -- Laws and legislation.
Gay liberation movement -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.

did.unittitle: Series I. Lesbian and Gay Rights

did.abstract: Highlights: 1977 letters to President and first Lady Carter, and reply from Margaret Midge Costanza, Assistant to the President 1977 letter to Supreme Court 1978 letter to Jerry Falwell other letter to Catholic and other religious leaders over the years, written as a Christian concerned about gay rights 1979 letter to Ann Landers 1979 letter from U.S. Rep. Matt McHugh on abortion rights 1980 letter from INS re: "homosexual aliens" 1981 letters to and from Dianne Feinstein, Mayor of San Francisco 1981 letters to and from Senator Patrick Moynihan about Family Protection Act correspondence with Cornell chapter of Phi Kappa Psi fraternity

Highlights: 1985 letter to Morley Safer, "60 Minutes" 1986 letter to Dan Rather, CBS News 1988 letter to Ithaca Times, and State Sen. James Seward 1995 letters to and from Fairfax County School Board, Virginia

did.unittitle: Cornell Gay Scholarship Offer, Cornell Libraries, 1981-1982, 1982-1992
did.abstract: Includes handwritten note from Barbara Gittings, then coordinator of the Gay Task Force of the American Library Association: "Thanks for sending stuff on the fascinating story of the gay scholarship and the gay books fund! Do please let me know the final outcome. - Barbara Also, 1982 letter from Michael Ellis sent to 14 faculty and staff members, including University Librarian Louis Martin, along with American Library Association's Gay Bibliography

did.unittitle: "Homosexuality and Social Justice," Archdiocese of San Francisco, 1982
Ed McKeown re: discrimination at Cornell, 1984-1985
CUGALA (Cornell University Gay and Lesbian Association) correspondence, 1980-1993
Lesbian and Gay Faculty and Staff at Cornell, 1990
Adding Sexual Orientation to Equal Educational and Employment Opportunity Statement, May, 1990
Correspondence with Human Rights Campaign Fund re: job, 1988
GLAAD (Gay and Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation), 1991
New York State Lesbian/Gay Lobby/How to Lobby, 1989
NGLTF Campus Project (National Gay and Lesbian Task Force), 1994
Senior Action in a Gay Environment, 1994-1995
PFLAG (Parents and Friends of Lesbians and Gays), 1984-1992
Project Coordinator, AIDS Risk Reduction, Cornell, ca. 1987-989
Gay/Lesbian/Bisexual Mental Health Conference, Cornell University, 1990
Cornell Panel Discussion on AIDS: Connecting Science and Public Policy, 1988
"AIDS: A Resource for the Cornell Community," Amy Amelkin, 1988

did.abstract: Planned Parenthood of Tompkins County, Tompkins County Community College, Hospicare
Planned Parenthood of Tompkins County, Tompkins County Community College, et al.

did.unittitle: Tompkins County Mental Health Association (TCMHA) Homophobia Training/Education, 1990
AIDSWork of Tompkins County Benefit, April 20, 1991

AASECT Conference (American Association of Sex Educators, Counselors, and Therapists), June 6-10, 1984
